title: What exactly ARE the evas
wikitext:
[]: What exactly ARE the evas?             Quote Post by Kye7 » Wed Oct 05, 2011 3:10 pm Been pondering this for a while now.. I know they're born (how?) from Lillith/Adam, but I'm still confused I'm not sure to think of them as monsters, robots, or hybrids.. See, apparently they're 'angels in body armor that restrains them', right? I believe Ritsuko said this after Unit-01 devoured Zuriel. So that would make them monsters with protection that allow humans to control them. But I don't know to think of them as robots, as seen in the Eva-graveyard, you can see many dead/failed Eva's with A HELMET... Is this helmet PART of their body? or was it added on later? I apologize if my sentence fluency is horrid, I'm at school, distracted, and these keyboard hurt my hands. E/ I will continue adding posts to this, I'm really curious!

[]:             Quote Post by Stryker » Wed Oct 05, 2011 3:33 pm Does anyone bother reading these anymore? Or is it not satisfactory for them?

[]: Saved me more than a couple times at least. B)             Quote Post by Alaska Slim » Wed Oct 05, 2011 4:07 pm Search function is your friend.

[]:             Quote Post by SaVaGe » Wed Oct 05, 2011 4:33 pm Think if people found God, harnessed his/her power, and created our own species, modified with all sorts of technologies. That's from a surface standpoint. Read the wiki page if you want to get deeper into it.

[]:             Quote Post by Kye7 » Wed Oct 05, 2011 7:33 pm Stryker wrote: Does anyone bother reading these anymore? Or is it not satisfactory for them?

[]: It's pretty laid out there. Bl             Quote Post by Alaska Slim » Wed Oct 05, 2011 7:39 pm ^ From the wiki: Evas are referred to as "copies" of Adam, and, in the Classified Information files, by the awkward phrase "clonal regenerations" (in reference to the "Adam Revival Project", or Project E).

[]:             Quote Post by Nice Hat » Wed Oct 05, 2011 8:06 pm EVA-01 was made by sucking material out of Lilith. Since Lilith's legs do not have enough volume for 01's whole body, either Lilith was recovering biomass or the rest was cloned from it. EVA-00 and EVA-02 and all the prototypes were cloned by using tissue from Adam. It's reasonable to assume this tissue was found in chunks after Adam exploded. I doubt they ran the embryo (or Tabris, which is a hybrid) for samples. Come to think of it, I have two questions concerning the EVAs. When 01 awakens, we hear her heartbeat. I've been always on the fence for this. On one side, it's impossible that a living organism did not live without a circulatory system and so, it must be a metaphor, however later when EVA is on the cage, we hear a drumming beat which we never had heard before, as if it's true that the heart beats now. Which one is it? And if EVA had no beating heart before, how the hell does it survive? LCL bathing? Electronic circuits? PVM (it's magic!) properties? It cannot be cybernetics, because the same system probably applies for the aquarium Reis so LCL / PVM? Unless the heart always beats, even if the soul is dormant / not present (which is even weirder). Was EVA-02's blue blood ever explained in some lower level of canon?

[]:             Quote Post by Azathoth » Wed Oct 05, 2011 8:35 pm Nice Hat wrote: And if EVA had no beating heart before, how the hell does it survive?

[]:             Quote Post by bobbyfischer's ghost » Wed Oct 05, 2011 8:45 pm Nice Hat wrote: When 01 awakens, we hear her heartbeat. I've been always on the fence for this. On one side, it's impossible that a living organism did not live without a circulatory system and so, it must be a metaphor, however later when EVA is on the cage, we hear a drumming beat which we never had heard before, as if it's true that the heart beats now. Which one is it? And if EVA had no beating heart before, how the hell does it survive? LCL bathing? Electronic circuits? PVM (it's magic!) properties? It cannot be cybernetics, because the same system probably applies for the aquarium Reis so LCL / PVM? Unless the heart always beats, even if the soul is dormant / not present (which is even weirder).

[]:             Quote Post by Kye7 » Wed Oct 05, 2011 9:05 pm bobbyfischer's ghost wrote: eva's are incomplete without an S2 organ that's why unit one took it from the angle in episode 19 .It moves because of yui's soul/heart, thus the heartbeat. [/url]

[]:             Quote Post by KingXanaduu » Wed Oct 05, 2011 9:23 pm Kye7 wrote: Oh good point! I forgot about this. Is the S2 engine.. an organ, like actual living tissue? Or is it literally, an engine with parts (like a car for example)?

[]:             Quote Post by Nice Hat » Thu Oct 06, 2011 5:34 am Azathoth wrote: That's what the cable is for, providing energy to fuel movement. All evidence is that Evas don't need to be alive the way we think of living - their consciousness isn't contained in their nervous system but in their core, and it's somewhat implied that they also move their bodies by AT-field usage rather than nervous/muscular shenanigans (Kyoko is entirely capable of shutting herself off from her own body, after all). I'd argue that the heartbeat starting is sort of literal: it's the Eva switching onto its own power source (I guess jump-starting the heart with AT-field energy or something? that doesn't make any sense, but whatever, neither does anything else)

[]:             Quote Post by gatotsu911 » Thu Oct 06, 2011 12:28 pm Obviously, an Eva is some sort of Freudian thing.

[]:             Quote Post by Stryker » Thu Oct 06, 2011 4:52 pm All you're missing, Gatotsu911, is the

[]:             Quote Post by GiftedPilot » Fri Oct 19, 2012 8:21 pm they are really all three: giant monsters, as they were born from monsters, pilots mothers, and a human, created in mans own image

[]:             Quote Post by Rj123541 » Sat Oct 20, 2012 12:07 am GiftedPilot wrote: they are really all three: giant monsters, as they were born from monsters, pilots mothers, and a human, created in God's own image

[]:             Quote Post by KnightmareX13 » Sat Oct 20, 2012 1:31 am giant meat puppets

[]:             Quote Post by GiftedPilot » Sat Oct 20, 2012 9:21 am I'm pretty sure Ritsuko says that it was created by man in mans own image, but its all the same. either way, they're still giant humanoid robots

[]:             Quote Post by Dream » Sat Oct 20, 2012 9:34 am GiftedPilot wrote: I'm pretty sure Ritsuko says that it was created by man in mans own image, but its all the same. either way, they're still giant humanoid robots
